_doc.what: Variant of config-sample.json showing EXTERNAL CLI EXECUTORS: any tier slot can point at a command-line agent (another vendor's CLI) instead of a native model name. Read config-sample.json first for the full key guide.
_doc.contract: A cli-shaped slot is GATHER-ONLY (cli_tier_gather_only): file hunts, scans, reviews, advisor consults run through it; cell execution never does. The prompt is piped via stdin (promptVia defaults to stdin); the command's stdout IS the digest, framed by the BEE_DIGEST delimiters per the Delegation contract's cli gather branch.
command_rules: The command must read the prompt from stdin (trailing '-' for codex exec, "$(cat)" for CLIs that only take argv prompts).
Prefer read-only/sandboxed flags (-s read-only, --sandbox, --mode plan) — a gather must not mutate the repo.
Long-running CLIs need their own timeout flags; bee does not babysit them.
Quote carefully: the command runs through a shell; bash -lc '...' keeps quoting sane for $(cat).
_doc.mixing: You can mix shapes freely per slot: here claude.extraction stays a native model (cheap + fast), while generation/review fan out to external CLIs, and codex.review uses the codex CLI itself. null slots stay prompt-budget.

examples_shown.claude.generation: Gemini via the agy CLI, plan mode, sandboxed, 30m print timeout.
examples_shown.claude.review: Claude Opus via the opencode CLI, plan agent.
examples_shown.codex.review: codex exec read-only with high reasoning effort, prompt from stdin ('-').
